text: I routinely give up my free time so that others can get what they need.
I find deep satisfaction in making personal sacrifices for the benefit of my community.
I believe my purpose is best fulfilled when I am contributing to someone else's well-being.
I tend to put other people's needs ahead of my own, even when it costs me.
I feel that my energy is best spent helping others reach their goals.
I hold the view that a life lived for others is the most meaningful kind of life.
When resources are scarce, I instinctively direct them toward other people first.
I find that volunteering my time for others gives me more fulfillment than personal leisure.
I would rather go without something I want so that someone else can have what they need.
I believe that putting myself last is a natural expression of how I value others.
I often skip meals or sleep to help someone who is counting on me.
I feel most aligned with my values when I am serving a purpose bigger than myself.
I regularly redirect my personal budget toward helping people around me.
I find that taking care of others' needs before my own feels right.
I believe the best use of my talents is to benefit other people.

I hold the view that self-sacrifice is one of the highest forms of contribution.
When I have extra money, my first thought is who else could use it.
I feel uneasy spending on luxuries for myself when others around me are struggling.

I feel that my personal comfort matters less than the comfort of those around me.
